sutta: monks the sangha if it wish may turn over the ball to a lay disciple who is possessed of 8 qualities of what aid he goes about to bring loss to the monks he goes about to harm them he goes about to evict them from their dwellings he reviles and defames them he causes this union between monk and monk he speaks in this praise of the buddha he speaks in this praise of dhamma he speaks in this praise of the sangha monks the sangha evid may turn down the bowl of a lay disciple who is possessed of these 8 qualities now this uh concerns have been rule the buddha said that if a lay person does any 1 of these 8 things he talks bad about the monk he harms harms them in some way in their reputation or something or he he tries to chase them out of their dwelling or he causes disharmony between manga and monk or his he speaks bad about the buddha dharma of sangha and for any 1 of these reasons among the the sangha if it wishes can decide to turn over the bowl to this lay disciples that means if the lay disciples this lay disciple wants to make an offering to the monks they can turn their bowl upside down so that they don't accept his offerings for her offerings this is 1 of the things the buddha allowed if certainly persons they are harmful to the monk or to the sangha then the the monk of the sangha can refuse to accept offerings to that from that person if the monk refuses to accept offerings for that from that person then that person has no chance to make marina and that person will suffer for it the next
